# Recovery Requirements Checklist: SaneGit Command Assistant

**Purpose**: Validate recovery and diagnostic requirement quality for `check`, `fix`, `undo`, and `wtf` workflows before implementation review
**Created**: 2026-04-21
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are explicit acceptance scenarios defined for `check` in addition to `fix`, `undo`, and `wtf`? [Gap, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-005] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK002 Are the failure-state classes covered by recovery requirements complete across conflicts, bad commits, accidental changes, failed CI, detached HEAD, and branch drift? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK003 Are the seven `wtf` diagnostic checks fully enumerated in requirements without leaving any implicit repository-state probe unspecified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for what `wtf` should do when no faults are found, rather than only when faults are detected? [Gap, Spec §FR-022] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements explicit about whether `wtf` only recommends a fix path or may initiate a repair handoff to `fix` directly? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-022] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK006 Are recovery requirements complete for repositories that are not initialized, given that this edge case is listed but not tied to a specific recovery flow? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: FAIL

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is “most urgent faults” defined with objective prioritization criteria so implementations can order `wtf` findings consistently? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 3] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK008 Is “fix-oriented recommendation” specific enough to distinguish between informational guidance, a recommended command, and an actionable remediation plan? [Clarity, Spec §FR-022] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK009 Is “degrade gracefully” defined with observable output expectations for skipped optional diagnostics such as GitHub CLI and remote metadata checks? [Clarity, Spec §FR-023] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK010 Are “common git failure states” defined precisely enough to bound `fix` and `undo` scope for reviewers and implementers? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK011 Is the boundary between `check` and `wtf` clear enough that reviewers can tell when a risk belongs to pre-integration analysis versus panic-button diagnosis? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-021] Result: PASS

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do User Story 3, FR-021 to FR-023, and SC-008 describe the same `wtf` outcome without conflicting expectations about diagnosis, prioritization, and remediation? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023, Spec §SC-008] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK013 Are recovery-flow output expectations consistent with the universal output structure used across the CLI? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Spec §UX-002] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK014 Do recovery edge cases align with the independent test definition, or are there states listed in one section but missing from the other? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK015 Are optional remote diagnostics in `wtf` consistent with the broader assumption that local planning should remain useful when network calls fail? [Consistency, Spec §FR-023, Spec §Assumptions] Result: PASS

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK016 Can SC-008 be objectively measured with a clear definition of “correct top-priority diagnosis” across multi-fault states? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK017 Are recovery success criteria complete enough to measure `check`, `fix`, `undo`, and `wtf` independently rather than relying on a shared blended outcome? [Gap, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-008] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK018 Do recovery requirements specify measurable expectations for when a recommendation is considered actionable rather than merely descriptive? [Measurability, Spec §FR-022, Spec §SC-008] Result: FAIL

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are primary recovery scenarios defined for all four commands in User Story 3, including an explicit `check` scenario? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §User Story 3] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK020 Are alternate-path requirements defined for repositories with only one fault versus multiple simultaneous faults in `wtf`? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-022] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK021 Are exception-flow requirements defined for unavailable GitHub CLI, missing CI history, missing `origin/main`, or unavailable remote metadata during diagnosis? [Coverage, Spec §FR-023, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK022 Are rollback and safety requirements defined for cases where `undo` would discard unpushed commits or uncommitted work? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: PASS

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are requirements explicit about how `wtf` should report detached HEAD together with another in-progress state like merge or rebase? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-021, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements defined for contradictory or overlapping findings, such as unresolved conflicts during a rebase with a dirty working tree? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-022] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK025 Are requirements clear about how recovery flows should behave when binary files or very large changes reduce explanation quality but not necessarily diagnostic confidence? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases] Result: FAIL

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are performance expectations defined for `wtf`, given that it adds seven parallel diagnostics to the recovery surface? [Gap, Spec §FR-021, Spec §PR-001, Spec §PR-002] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK027 Are observability requirements specific enough to ensure recovery and diagnostic outcomes can be audited without exposing secrets or noisy raw command output? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-023] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK028 Are plain-English wording requirements sufficient to constrain fault summaries, urgency labels, and remediation text across recovery commands? [Non-Functional, Spec §UX-001, Spec §UX-002] Result: PASS

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 Are external dependency assumptions for GitHub CLI and remote metadata availability documented clearly enough for reviewers to judge what is optional versus required? [Assumption, Spec §FR-023, Spec §Assumptions] Result: PASS
- [x] CHK030 Is the dependency on merge-queue prediction sufficiently documented so reviewers know what `wtf` must still report if predictor input is absent or inconclusive? [Dependency, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-023] Result: PASS

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK031 Does the spec avoid conflict between “offers to fix it” in the feature intent and “offer a fix-oriented recommendation” in the current requirements? [Conflict, Spec §Input, Spec §FR-022] Result: FAIL
- [x] CHK032 Are placeholder comments in the recovery-related sections removed or clearly non-authoritative so they do not create ambiguity about checklist readiness? [Ambiguity, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Requirements, Spec §Success Criteria] Result: FAIL

## Notes

- Use this checklist during PR review of recovery and diagnostic requirement changes.
- Record any gaps directly against the relevant spec section before implementation begins.
- Validation pass complete: checklist reviewed with explicit per-item PASS/FAIL outcomes.
